testcase.name: testLoginNullPassword
testcase.failure: class java.lang.IllegalArgumentException class org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: Unexpected exception type thrown, expected: <java.lang.IllegalArgumentException> but was: <org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem> at org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertionFailureBuilder.build(AssertionFailureBuilder.java:151) at org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertThrows.assertThrows(AssertThrows.java:67) at org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertThrows.assertThrows(AssertThrows.java:35) at org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertThrows(Assertions.java:3115) at com.auth.service.AuthServiceTest.testLoginNullPassword(AuthServiceTest.java:440) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568) at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1511) at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1511) Caused by: org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'matches' method: passwordEncoder.matches(null, "encoded"); -> at com.auth.service.AuthService.login(AuthService.java:89) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. passwordEncoder.matches("", ""); -> at com.auth.service.AuthServiceTest.testLoginNullPassword(AuthServiceTest.java:437) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.auth.service.AuthService.login(AuthService.java:89) at com.auth.service.AuthServiceTest.lambda$8(AuthServiceTest.java:441) at org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertThrows.assertThrows(AssertThrows.java:53) ... 6 more

testcase.name: testUpdatePasswordNullNewPassword
testcase.error: org.mockito.exceptions.misusing.PotentialStubbingProblem: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'encode' method: passwordEncoder.encode(null); -> at com.auth.service.AuthService.updatePassword(AuthService.java:102) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. passwordEncoder.encode(""); -> at com.auth.service.AuthServiceTest.testUpdatePasswordNullNewPassword(AuthServiceTest.java:479) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.auth.service.AuthService.updatePassword(AuthService.java:102) at com.auth.service.AuthServiceTest.testUpdatePasswordNullNewPassword(AuthServiceTest.java:483) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568) at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1511) at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1511)

testcase.name: testRegisterNullFields
